latency: event-driven Notify replaces 2ms polling

samples_collector:
- Add data_notify: Arc<tokio::sync::Notify> field
- FALLBACK_INTERVAL=10ms (was READ_INTERVAL=2ms)
- new_with_callback() now accepts Arc<Notify>

mod.rs (receive_with_callback):
- Create Arc<Notify>, pass to SamplesCollector
- Wire TransferNotifier{callback: notify_one, max_interval_samples: 48}
  so flows_rx fires callback after each MIO poll (≤0.5ms after packet arrival)

Effect: avg wakeup latency 1ms→<0.5ms; patchbox total ≈ 4ms (was 7ms)

commit.message:
chore: release v1.51.0 (#285)

* fix: update E2E nav selectors for module-driven ActivityBar buttons (#252)

* fix: update E2E nav selectors for module-driven ActivityBar buttons

The §29 Phase 3 migration (8a81e9e) changed nav items from <a> links
to <button> elements, but E2E tests still used getByRole('link').
This caused all navigation-related E2E tests to time out at 31.5s.

Changes:
- ActivityBar: add aria-current="page" to active nav button (a11y)
- navigation.ts: update selectors from role=link to role=button
- navigation.ts: add Sessions to NAV_ITEMS, reorder by module sort order
- p6-knowledge, p7-self-improvement, p8-escalation: fix nav selectors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve strict mode violation in E2E setup wizard Copy button test

The OAuth step now shows two Copy buttons (homepage URL + callback URL).
Use .first() to avoid Playwright strict mode violation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.5.10 settings auditability — log module settings changes to audit log (#251)

Add audit logging for module settings changes per spec §29.5.10:

- Register 4 new audit event types in AuditEventCatalog:
  module.settings.updated, module.settings.reset, module.enabled, module.disabled
- Inject IAuditLog into ModuleController
- Log settings updates (global + project-scoped) with previous/new values
- Log settings resets with cleared key list
- Log module enable/disable actions
- Secret values redacted as '***' in all audit payloads
- Audit logging is best-effort (try/catch) — failures don't fail the request
- Project-scoped updates resolve previous values per-key via GetForProjectAsync

Spec updated: §29.5.10 marked as delivered with implementation details.
Phase 4 checklist updated.

Tests: 9 new tests in ModuleControllerAuditTests covering:
- Settings update audit with previous/new values
- Secret redaction in audit payloads
- Masked secret skip (no audit for unchanged secrets)
- Project-scoped settings audit
- Settings reset audit
- Enable/disable audit
- No audit on validation failure
- Audit failure resilience (request still succeeds)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.5.9 settings provenance tracking — show value source in Settings UI (#254)

Display provenance badges (Default/Global) next to module settings fields
so users can see which layer a resolved value comes from.

- Add Default property to C# ModuleSettingsFieldSchema for manifest defaults
- Compute provenance client-side from stored values vs schema defaults
- Show effective values including defaults when no stored value exists
- Update spec §29.5.9 with implementation details

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.3.9 prefetch hints — low-priority module bundle prefetching (#257)

Add PrefetchManager that accepts manifest-level prefetch hints from modules
declaring which other modules they commonly navigate to. Prefetched bundles
are fetched at low priority via requestIdleCallback (setTimeout fallback)
with concurrency control (max 2 concurrent, max 5 queued).

Changes:
- New: prefetchManager.ts — manages loader registry, queue, budget, cancel
- types.ts: add prefetchModules?: string[] to ModuleManifest + DTO + validation
- coreModules.ts: extract import fns for loader registration; Chat prefetches
  Agents and Sessions
- ModuleContainer.tsx: trigger prefetch when module reaches Ready/Mounted
- moduleRegistry.ts: cancel queued prefetches on disable (both own and hinted)
- 16 new tests covering queue, dedup, cancel, budget, concurrency, failure

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.7.7 per-module performance monitoring (#259)

Add frontend-only metrics collector that tracks per-module lifecycle
events (init timing, mount count, suspend/resume, faults) and surfaces
them in the Settings → Modules UI.

New files:
- moduleMetrics.ts — singleton collector with timer API, ring buffer,
  onChange subscriptions
- ModulePerformancePanel.tsx — diagnostic UI with metric grid and
  collapsible event log
- moduleMetrics.test.ts — 19 unit tests

Instrumentation:
- ModuleContainer: init timing (wraps onInit), mount tracking with
  StrictMode dedup via compound key ref, fault/dispose events
- moduleRegistry: suspend, resume, dispose, render-crash events

Spec §29.7.7 updated from MAY/planned to implemented.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: mask secret fields in GetProjectSettings response (#256) (#260)

The GetProjectSettings endpoint returned decrypted secret values to the
client. Now checks ModuleSettingsFieldSchema.Secret for each key and
returns '***' for secret fields, consistent with GetAllAsync masking.

Added test: GetProjectSettings_MasksSecretFields

Closes #256

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.7.8 memory leak detection in dev mode

Track module memory footprint before init and after dispose + GC delay.
Warn in console and Performance panel if retained memory exceeds 50 MB.

- Uses performance.measureUserAgentSpecificMemory() with legacy fallback
- Versioned snapshots prevent race conditions on rapid remount
- Gated behind import.meta.env.DEV — tree-shaken in production
- 7 new tests (26 total in moduleMetrics suite)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.3 module visibility per project (#263)

Add per-project module visibility configuration. Projects can specify
which modules are hidden from the activity bar. Hidden modules remain
accessible via direct URL — only the nav icon is suppressed.

Backend:
- New IModuleVisibilityStore interface (Domain)
- SqliteModuleVisibilityStore with transactional replace (Infrastructure)
- Migration 014: module_visibility table
- GET/PUT /api/module/visibility/project/{projectId} endpoints
- Audit logging for visibility changes
- DI registration + singleton allowlist

Frontend:
- API client functions for visibility endpoints
- moduleStore tracks hiddenByProject per project ID
- ActivityBar filters out hidden modules
- Retry-on-failure: transient fetch errors don't cache false positives

Tests:
- 6 unit tests for SqliteModuleVisibilityStore
- 5 unit tests for ModuleController visibility endpoints
- Architecture singleton allowlist updated

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.5 cache keying by project (#265)

Add project-scoped state caching for modules to prevent cross-project
data leakage. Module state (list data, filters, scroll positions, etc.)
is keyed by moduleId+projectId and automatically switches when the
active project changes.

New SDK hook: useProjectScopedState(key, initialValue) — works like
useState but the value is keyed by moduleId+projectId+key. Previous
project's cached values are preserved for instant restore on switch-back.

Implementation uses nested Map<moduleId, Map<projectId, Map<key, value>>>
to avoid composite-string key pitfalls. Cache entries are cleaned up on
module dispose (both inline and via registry._disposeSuspended()).

Files:
- src/frontend/src/modules/projectCache.ts (new) — cache singleton
- src/frontend/src/modules/hooks.ts — useProjectScopedState hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/index.ts — barrel export for hook
- src/frontend/src/modules/moduleRegistry.ts — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/modules/ModuleContainer.tsx — clearModule on dispose
- src/frontend/src/test/projectCache.test.ts (new) — 12 tests
- docs/spec/29-module-system.md — updated §29.8.5 to reflect impl

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: update §29 Phase 4 checklist with completed items

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.8.6 project deletion cleanup (#267)

Implement project-scoped data cleanup when a project is deleted.

Backend:
- Add DeleteForProjectAsync to IModuleSettingsStore, IPluginSettingsStore,
  and IModuleVisibilityStore domain interfaces
- Implement in SQLite stores with LIKE-escaped project ID matching
- New IProjectCleanupService orchestrator calls all three stores
- DELETE /api/module/project/{projectId} endpoint with 207 on partial failure
- Audit logging for cleanup events

Frontend:
- cleanupProjectModuleData() API client function
- projectCache.clearProject() already existed for in-memory cleanup

Safety:
- SQL LIKE wildcards in projectId are escaped to prevent cross-project deletion
- Partial store failures return HTTP 207 with details (not masked as 200 OK)
- Each store cleanup is independent — one failure doesn't block others

The project system does not yet have a project entity with CRUD operations.
This provides the cleanup building blocks ready to wire when project
deletion is added.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: mark delivered P1 items with ✅ in §29 spec headers

Mark §29.2.6 (Keep-Alive), §29.4.8 (SDK Utility Hooks), §29.6.6
(Navigation Guards), §29.9.2 (Status Bar), §29.9.3 (Command Palette),
and §29.13.5 (Dev Mode) as delivered — all were completed in Phase 2/3
but spec section headers weren't updated.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.2.9 dispose ordering — reverse nav order on shutdown

Add disposeAll() to ModuleRegistry that disposes modules in reverse
navigation.order (highest first, core modules last). Wired to pagehide
event in App.tsx. Includes idempotency guard (isShutdown) to prevent
double-disposal when React unmount runs after disposeAll(). Respects
BFCache by checking PageTransitionEvent.persisted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: §29.3.10 bundle integrity — SHA-256 ETag and dev-mode verification

Backend (ModuleController.GetBundle):
- Compute SHA-256 of bundle file contents, return as ETag header
- Handle If-None-Match for 304 Not Modified (proper EntityTagHeaderValue parsing)
- Read file once to guarantee hash/body consistency (bundles capped at 2 MB)

Frontend (modules/bundleIntegrity.ts):
- fetchBundleWithIntegrity() fetches bundle and verifies SHA-256 in dev mode
- Uses SubtleCrypto.digest for client-side hash computation
- Skips verification in production (browser HTTP cache uses ETag natively)
- Re-exported from api/client.ts for consumer convenience

Tests:
- 6 new vitest tests covering: hash match, mismatch rejection, HTTP errors,
  missing ETag, URL encoding, AbortSignal passthrough

Spec: §29.3.10 updated with implementation details, marked ✅

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: Discord plugin UX and settings double-encoding

- Rename "Guild ID" to "Server ID" in plugin settings label/description
  (Discord users say "server", not "guild")
- Update all user-facing messages: error strings, DM redirect text,
  log messages now use "server" instead of "guild"
- Fix settings double-encoding bug: string values were wrapped in JSON
  quotes by JsonSerializer.Serialize(), causing progressive corruption
  on each save (quotes → escaped quotes → deeper escaping)
- Add §29.4.9 cancellation primitive tests (5 tests) and mark spec ✅

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: persist DataProtection key ring across Docker rebuilds (#269)

AddDataProtection() was called without PersistKeysToFileSystem(),
so the key ring regenerated on every container rebuild. This caused:
- Session cookies to become undecryptable (logout)
- Encrypted OAuth secrets in system_config to fail Unprotect()
- Users prompted to re-run the GitHub OAuth setup wizard

Now explicitly persists keys to ~/.aspnet/DataProtection-Keys,
which is volume-mounted as matrix-keys in docker-compose.yml.
Includes a fallback guard for edge cases where UserProfile is empty.

Also recovered the old key (99f8aade) from the stale
the-matrix_matrix-keys volume into the active docker_matrix-keys
volume, so existing encrypted secrets will decrypt again after
container restart.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: implement §29.7.5 timer & subscription cleanup (#270)

* feat: implement §29.5.8 settings migration on version change

When a module's settingsSchema changes between versions, the host now
detects the version change and migrates settings:

- Track module versions in module_state.version (migration 015)
- On startup, compare manifest version to stored version
- Remove stored values incompatible with new schema constraints
  (type changes, out-of-range numbers, removed select options)
- Handle both global and project-scoped settings (project:{id}:{key})
- Preserve unmapped fields as-is (keys not in new schema)
- Do NOT eagerly persist defaults — preserves §29.5.9 provenance
- On failure: log warning, skip version update, retry next startup
- Secret values skipped (masked, can't validate)

Domain: IModuleSettingsStore gains GetVersionAsync, SetVersionAsync,
DeleteKeyAsync. Infrastructure: SqliteModuleSettingsStore implements them.
ModuleDiscoveryService runs migration after scan.

22 new tests (16 migration behavior + 6 store-level).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: implement §29.7.5 timer & subscription cleanup

Add dev-mode resource tracker for module lifecycle leak detection:

- moduleResourceTracker singleton tracks resources per module
  (timeouts, intervals, event listeners, SignalR subscriptions)
- signalR.on transparently wrapped in ModuleContainer init context
  and ModuleContextProvider component context for automatic tracking
- SDK hooks: useTrackedTimeout, useTrackedInterval,
  useTrackedEventListener for opt-in component-level tracking
- checkLeaks() logs warning after onDispose(), forceCleanup()
  clears remaining resources as safety net
- Integrated into all dispose paths: ModuleContainer unmount,
  moduleRegistry.disposeAll(), _disposeSuspended()
- 25 new tests covering tracker lifecycle, leak detection,
  force-cleanup, error handling, and idempotency

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add deprecationManager singleton to the module SDK that provides
infrastructure for deprecating fields, functions, and hooks:

- register() declares deprecations with version metadata

- wrapFunction/wrapProperty/wrapHook create transparent wrappers
- Zero-overhead in production (all methods are no-ops)

17 new tests covering registration, deduplication, wrapping, and

The Settings → Modules tab already displays version, lifecycle state with

* fix: Docker version defaults to Directory.Build.props instead of 0.0.0-dev (#274)

Previously docker compose builds always showed version 0.0.0-dev because
the BUILD_VERSION arg defaulted to that value. Now the Dockerfile copies
Directory.Build.props into the build context and reads the version from
it when BUILD_VERSION is not explicitly set. Explicit BUILD_VERSION still
works for CI overrides.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: replace blocking GetAwaiter().GetResult() with await in test

Resolves xUnit1031 warning in InboundEvent_SinkPublishFailure_DoesNotCrashOperator
test. Blocking task operations in async tests can cause deadlocks.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: eliminate 50 React act() warnings across frontend tests

Components with async useEffect hooks (calling mocked API functions returning
mockResolvedValue) triggered state updates via .then(setState) in microtasks
outside act(). Fixed by adding await act(async () => {}) after render calls
to flush pending microtasks within an act() scope.

Files: ChatView, DiscordPlugin, KnowledgeView, ModuleContainer, PluginView,
SelfImprovementView, SignalRProvider, useSlashCommands

Before: 50 act() warnings across 7 components
After: 0 warnings, all 599 tests passing

Cover List, Get, Enable, Disable, GetBundle (with ETag/304),
GetHealth (with route conflict detection), GetSettings,
UpdateSettings (validation: type checks, min/max, select options,
required fields, secret masking, multi-error, atomicity),
GetProjectSettings (secret masking, null omission),

Extend existing AgentCommandHandlerTests with coverage for:

- parentBranch absent → null passthrough

commit.message:
[next-mapper] feat: implement mapper 282 (J.Y. Company ASIC outer-bank variant) (#1728)

* feat: implement mapper 282 (J.Y. Company ASIC outer-bank variant)

Mapper 282 is a J.Y. Company ASIC mapper used by J.Y. multicarts:
- 1997 Super HIK 18-in-1 (JY-101)
- 1997 Super HIK 21-in-1 (JY-105)
- 1998 Super HIK 5-in-1 (JY-114)
- Super 25-in-1 Final Fight (SC-128)

The mapper differs from mapper 281 only in the $D003 outer-bank register layout:
- PRG outer bank: bits 2:1 (PP) of $D003 select 256 KiB PRG windows
- CHR outer bank: bit 5 (L) controls mode:
  - L=0: bit 0 (c) selects 256 KiB CHR window
  - L=1: M/C bits select 512 KiB CHR window

All other J.Y. Company ASIC registers (PRG/CHR banking modes, IRQ,
mirroring, hardware multiplier) are identical to mapper 281.

Adds 20 unit tests covering:
- Registration
- Power-on state
- PRG outer bank bits 1-2 (mapper 282-specific)
- CHR outer bank L/M/C/c logic (mapper 282-specific)
- Mirroring (standard and extended)
- IRQ prescaler
- Hardware multiplier
- Snapshot/restore roundtrip

Closes #1293

Fix remote shell state: prompt detection wins over idle timer

Remote shell sessions showed 'active' when sitting at bash prompt
because: content hash changed slightly on each poll (ANSI jitter) →
idle_seconds reset to ~0 → prompt check required idle >= 1.0 → lost
to 'idle < 3.0 → active' rule.

Fix: prompt detection is now authoritative — if a prompt pattern
matches the last lines, the session is 'waiting' regardless of how
recently the content hash changed. A visible prompt IS the ground
truth for 'waiting for input'.

This complements commit 6321f19 (TUI sessions use tui_busy metadata)
to give correct state for both TUI and shell remote sessions.

commit.message:
fix: title generation prompt leaking into chat (#172) (#173)

* fix(#172): mark needsHistoryReplay after title generation to flush KV cache

generateOnce() sends the title prompt into the live LiteRT conversation session,
permanently writing the prompt and its response into the KV cache. Without
a session reset, the model retains the strict title-formatting instruction
and emits it verbatim on the next user message.

Setting needsHistoryReplay = true immediately after generateOnce() ensures the
next sendMessage() call resets the session via updateSystemPrompt() and re-injects
clean conversation history, washing the title artefacts out of the KV cache.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(#172): set needsHistoryReplay before generateOnce to close race window

Move flag set to before the generateOnce() call so any concurrent sendMessage()
that slips past the isGenerating guard still gets a history replay. The catch
block comment also clarifies the flag stays set on failure since the KV cache
may be partially dirty from the prompt write.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use METRICS_GITHUB_TOKEN for fixture PR creation and auto-merge (#54)

* fix: correct period-filtered merged-PR counts in repo table

Three issues caused the merged-PR column to always show ≤10:

1. The applyFilter JS updated all cells (including 'All Time') from
   CHART_DATA.allPRDetails, which falls back to pullRequestDetails
   (max 10 entries) when mergedPRDates is absent from cached data.
   Fix: for period='all' restore cells from the data-merged-prs
   attribute, which always holds the true all-time API total.

2. repoCounts was keyed by p.repo (original API casing) but looked
   up via row.dataset.repoName which is explicitly lowercased in the
   HTML. Fix: lowercase the key when building repoCounts.

3. Moved repoCounts accumulation outside the per-row loop so it is
   built once (O(n+m)) rather than once per row (O(n*m)).

Also updates the stale JSDoc on RepoMetrics.mergedPRDates which said
'single cheap API call / last ~100 PRs' but the function actually
paginates up to 10 pages for ~1000 PRs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use listForAuthenticatedUser for user-mode repo discovery

GET /users/{username}/repos only returns repos where the user is an
explicit per-repo collaborator. It silently omits every repo in orgs
the user is a member of (even public ones), because org membership
alone is not enough for that endpoint.

Fix: when ownerType is 'user' and the token belongs to the same user
we are collecting for, switch to GET /user/repos (listForAuthenticatedUser
with type='all'). That endpoint explicitly includes repos accessible
via org membership, so orgs like devex-metrics and devops-actions are
now discovered correctly.

Fallback behaviour:
- If users.getAuthenticated() returns a different login, the original
  listForUser path is used (collecting for a third-party user).
- If getAuthenticated() throws with 401/403 (GitHub App token with no
  user context), fall back silently to listForUser.
- Any other error is logged as a warning before falling back.

Tests: 4 new cases cover the auth-match path, case-insensitive match,
mismatch fallback, and getAuthenticated-throws fallback. Org mode is
also asserted to never call getAuthenticated().

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use METRICS_GITHUB_TOKEN for fixture PR creation and auto-merge

GITHUB_TOKEN cannot create PRs due to org policy; METRICS_GITHUB_TOKEN
is a PAT with the necessary repo scope.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: AWS ARM64 ENA connectivity test via extra-nic injection

Adds an end-to-end test that verifies extra-nic.npk injection gives
working ENA networking on AWS EC2 Graviton (t4g.small).

Test flow (scripts/test-aws-arm64.ts):
  1. Upload pre-built ARM64 image (with extra-nic injected) to S3
  2. ImportSnapshot → EBS snapshot → RegisterImage → ARM64 AMI
  3. Launch t4g.small (Graviton2) in locked-down security group
  4. Open port 80, poll http://<ip>/ for up to 10 minutes
  5. Any HTTP response = ENA is working = test passes
  6. Terminate + deregister AMI (always, via try/finally)

RouterOS first-boot with extra-nic.npk:
  - Installs extra-nic.npk (contains ena.ko) → reboots
  - ENA interface appears as ether1
  - WebFig comes up on port 80
  If we can reach WebFig, ENA works. If not within 10 min, injection failed.

GitHub Actions workflow (.github/workflows/test-aws-arm64.yml):
  - ubuntu-latest (Linux required for qemu-nbd injection step)
  - Installs qemu-utils, runs convert (inject), verifies injection via
    mount+ls, then runs the test script
  - workflow_dispatch only (manual trigger — test costs ~bash.02)
  - AWS secrets: AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, AWS_DEFAULT_REGION

The verify step in the workflow mounts the ext4 rootfs after injection
to confirm extra-nic-*.npk is actually present before uploading to AWS.
This catches injection failures early (before the 15-min import wait).

commit.message:
Make Multi module and add Offline support (#3)

* Modularize project and add market news feature

- Refactor project structure into a modular architecture, introducing `:core:network`, `:core:ui`, `:feature:exchangerate`, and `:feature:marketnews` modules.
- Move networking logic to `:core:network` and common UI components/theming to `:core:ui`.
- Migrate the exchange rate feature to its own library module `:feature:exchangerate`.
- Implement a new Market News feature in `:feature:marketnews` that fetches and displays news articles and sentiment data using the Alpha Vantage API.
- Create `MarketNewsRepository`, `MarketNewsViewModel`, and `MarketNewsScreen` to handle news data and UI state.
- Update `AppNavGraph` and `HomeScreen` to include navigation to the new Market News screen.
- Provide a `NetworkModule` in the `:app` module to configure Retrofit with `BuildConfig` constants.
- Add unit tests for `MarketNewsRepositoryImpl` and updated `ExchangeRateViewModel`.
- Update `settings.gradle.kts` and `build.gradle.kts` files to reflect the new module structure and dependencies.

* Refactor NetworkModule to split MarketApi into MarketNewsApi and ExchangeRateApi

- Replace `MarketApi` with `MarketNewsApi` and `ExchangeRateApi` in `NetworkModule`.
- Update Hilt provider functions to supply instances for `MarketNewsApi` and `ExchangeRateApi`.
- Update KDoc documentation to reflect the API service changes.

* Ignore unused exception variable in `MarketNewsRepositoryImpl`

- Replace `e` with `_` in the `catch` block of the date formatting logic to indicate the exception is intentionally unused.

* Refactor project structure to modularize the home feature

- Create a new `:feature:home` Android library module.
- Move `HomeScreen.kt` from the `:app` module to the new `:feature:home` module and update its package name.
- Update `AppNavGraph.kt` and `build.gradle.kts` in the `:app` module to reference the new home feature module.
- Add `appcompat` and `material` dependencies to `libs.versions.toml`.
- Reformat code in `AppNavGraph.kt` for improved readability.
- Include `:feature:home` in `settings.gradle.kts`.

* Remove boilerplate instrumented test from home feature

- Delete `ExampleInstrumentedTest.kt` from the `home` feature module.

* Upgrade build configuration and implement local storage for exchange rates

- Upgrade Java toolchain and target compatibility from version 11 to 21 across all modules.
- Migrate from `kapt` to `ksp` for dependency injection and annotation processing.
- Update Gradle to 8.7 and AGP to 8.6.0.
- Update Kotlin to 2.1.10 and various library versions including Navigation and Hilt Compose.
- Implement local data persistence for the exchange rate feature using Room:
    - Add `ExchangeRateDatabase`, `ExchangeRateDao`, and `ExchangeRateEntity`.
    - Provide Room database and DAO instances via `ExchangeRateModule`.
    - Add domain mapping functions to convert between entities and domain models.
- Add Room dependencies to `marketnews` and `exchangerate` feature modules.
- Remove hardcoded `org.gradle.java.home` from `gradle.properties`.

* Refactor database logic into a dedicated core module and implement offline-first caching for exchange rates

- Create `:core:database` module to centralize data persistence, including `MarketAnalyserDatabase`, `ExchangeRateEntity`, and `ExchangeRateDao`.
- Move exchange rate local data sources from `:feature:exchangerate` to `:core:database`.
- Update `ExchangeRateRepositoryImpl` to implement an offline-first strategy with a 5-minute cache TTL.
- Introduce `ExchangeRateMapper` to handle conversions between DTOs, entities, and domain models.
- Refactor Hilt modules to provide database dependencies from the core module instead of the feature module.
- Update `ExchangeRateRepositoryImplTest` to verify caching logic, including fresh cache hits, stale cache updates, and network failure fallbacks.
- Update `build.gradle.kts` and `settings.gradle.kts` to include and link the new `:core:database` module.

* Clean up `ExchangeRateDao` and update `DatabaseModule`

- Remove redundant `Companion` reference in `OnConflictStrategy.REPLACE` within `ExchangeRateDao`.
- Update `DatabaseModule` imports (preparation for providing `ExchangeRateDao`).

* Clean up AndroidManifest.xml in core:database

- Remove unused `android` namespace declaration.

* Remove `AppModule.kt` and update dependencies in `app/build.gradle.kts`

- Delete `AppModule.kt` as part of a refactoring effort to decentralize dependency injection.
- Add `:core:database` module dependency to `app/build.gradle.kts`.

* Refactor project architecture to multi-module and update build environment

- Upgrade JDK from 17 to 21 across GitHub Actions workflows and project documentation.
- Transition from a monolithic package structure to a multi-module architecture:
    - `app`: Handles navigation and Hilt entry point.
    - `core/network`: Centralizes Retrofit APIs and DTOs.
    - `core/database`: Manages Room entities, DAOs, and persistence logic.
    - `core/ui`: Contains shared themes and reusable Compose components.
    - `feature/*`: Encapsulates feature-specific logic (Exchange Rate, Market News).
- Implement an offline-first caching strategy in the data layer using Room with a 5-minute TTL.
- Add "Market News & Sentiment" feature documentation and API references.
- Update project dependencies in `README.md`, including AGP 8.6.0, KSP, and Room 2.6.1.
- Document key design decisions regarding module boundaries, mapping logic, and state management.

* Update network configuration and clean up `feature:home` module dependencies

- Reorder interceptors in `NetworkModule.kt` to ensure `HttpLoggingInterceptor` logs the request before the `apiKeyInterceptor` adds the API key.
- Remove unused Hilt and KSP plugins from `feature/home/build.gradle.kts`.
- Clean up `feature/home` dependencies by removing unused references to `:core:network`, Hilt, and testing libraries.
- Delete `ExampleUnitTest.kt` from the `feature:home` module.

* Clean up dependencies and remove boilerplate test in `core:database`

- Remove unused `androidx.core.ktx` and `androidx.appcompat` dependencies from `build.gradle.kts`.
- Remove unused testing dependencies (`junit`, `mockk`, `kotlinx-coroutines-test`) from `build.gradle.kts`.

* Delete `ExchangeRateRepositoryImpl.kt`

- Remove the implementation of `ExchangeRateRepository` that provided offline-first exchange rate data with a 5-minute cache strategy.

* Remove unused Room dependencies from `feature/marketnews`

- Remove `room.runtime`, `room.compiler`, and `room.ktx` from `build.gradle.kts`.

* Remove unused Room dependencies from `feature/exchangerate`

- Remove Room runtime, compiler, and KTX extensions from `build.gradle.kts`.

- Update `getExchangeRate` in `ExchangeRateRepositoryImpl` to rethrow `CancellationException`, ensuring proper coroutine lifecycle management.
- Add KDoc documentation explaining the necessity of rethrowing `CancellationException` to avoid returning stale results when a job is stopped.
